text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old turtle named Turtlesworth. Every morning he would wander into the dew-kissed meadows where butterflies flitted about and flowers swayed gently in the breeze. Among these blossoms he'd often seek the company of a delightful little bird named Ruby.

One dawn, after a particularly enchanting display, Ruby chirped brightly as she skipped across the field. "Good morning, Turtlesworth!" she sang.

"Ahh," said Turtlesworth softly. "Good morning, Ruby. How is your day today?"

"One hundred percent brilliant!" Ruby exclaimed.

"Turtlesworth wouldn't dream of anything less," replied the turtle, chuckling.

Together they made their way back toward Turtlesworth's pond. Along the way, Turtlesworth had an insight—he knew that today was not just any day. They were going to gather elderberries.

Elderberries were sacred berries of autumn – a potent tonic known for their magical properties and for soothing the soul. Turtlesworth
